revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''戸祭大塚古墳'''（とまつりおおつかこふん）は、[[栃木県]][[宇都宮市]]上戸祭町に存在する[[円墳]]である。通常は単に...'
revision.text:
'''戸祭大塚古墳'''（とまつりおおつかこふん）は、[[栃木県]][[宇都宮市]]上戸祭町に存在する[[円墳]]である。通常は単に'''大塚古墳'''と呼ぶ。
== 概要 == 大塚古墳は、径53.4メートル、高さ6.2メートルの2段築成の大型円墳である。1段目円墳の高さは3.4メートルで、この上に高さ2.8メートルの2段目円墳が乗っている。2段目円墳の頂上には径9メートルの平坦部があり、載頭円錐型の全国的にも珍しい美しい円墳として知られる。墳の周縁部には一部破損しているが北西側から東側にかけて幅約6.6メートル、深さ約1.3メートルほどの周溝が現存する。石室が盗掘され詳細は不明だが、玄室長5.17メートル、凝灰岩割石の平積み横穴式石室と推定されており、築造年代は[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]後半と考えられている。
[[1956年]]（[[昭和]]32年）[[6月30日]]、同じく市内の[[笹塚古墳]]とともに栃木県指定史跡に登録されている。
== 周辺環境 == 大塚古墳は宇都宮市北部の宇都宮丘陵の南部、[[戸祭山]]の北側の[[田川 (利根川水系)|田川]]西岸に立地する。周辺には当墳をはじめ、[[谷口山古墳]]や[[北山古墳群]]、[[瓦塚古墳群]]、[[長岡百穴古墳]]などの古代古墳群、また[[奈良時代]]の遺跡である[[水道山瓦窯跡]]などの古代遺跡が数多く認められており、[[豊郷まほろばの道]]が整備されている。
== アクセス == ;路線バス * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[宇都宮駅]]西口から ** [[関東自動車 (栃木県)|関東バス]] 戸祭台循環線に乗車約15分、「第二グリーンヒル北」下車徒歩10分。
;車 * [[国道119号]]（[[宇都宮環状道路|宮環]]）長岡町交差点を南進1分、もしくは同（[[日光街道]]）の上戸祭町交差点を東進1分の[[栃木県道157号下岡本上戸祭線|長岡街道]]から枝道を東進1分。
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧]] * [[北山古墳群]] * [[瓦塚古墳群]] * [[谷口山古墳]] * [[長岡百穴古墳]] * [[豊郷まほろばの道]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:とまつりおおつかこふん}} [[Category:栃木県の古墳]] [[Category:宇都宮市]]
{{Japanese-history-stub|とまつりおおつかこふん}}

'''戸祭大塚古墳'''（とまつりおおつかこふん）は、[[栃木県]][[宇都宮市]]上戸祭町に存在する[[円墳]]である。通常は単に'''大塚古墳'''と呼ぶ。
== 概要 == 大塚古墳は、径53.4メートル、高さ6.2メートルの2段築成の大型円墳である。1段目円墳の高さは3.4メートルで、この上に高さ2.8メートルの2段目円墳が乗っている。2段目円墳の頂上には径9メートルの平坦部があり、載頭円錐型の全国的にも珍しい美しい円墳として知られる。墳の周縁部には一部破損しているが北西側から東側にかけて幅約6.6メートル、深さ約1.3メートルほどの周溝が現存する。石室が盗掘され詳細は不明だが、玄室長5.17メートル、凝灰岩割石の平積み横穴式石室と推定されており、築造年代は[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]後半と考えられている。
[[1956年]]（[[昭和]]32年）[[6月30日]]、同じく市内の[[笹塚古墳]]とともに栃木県指定史跡に登録されている。
== 周辺環境 == 大塚古墳は宇都宮市北部の宇都宮丘陵の南部、[[戸祭山]]の北側の[[田川 (利根川水系)|田川]]西岸に立地する。周辺には当墳をはじめ、[[谷口山古墳]]や[[北山古墳群]]、[[瓦塚古墳群]]、[[長岡百穴古墳]]などの古代古墳群、また[[奈良時代]]の遺跡である[[水道山瓦窯跡]]などの古代遺跡が数多く認められており、[[豊郷まほろばの道]]が整備されている。
== アクセス == ;路線バス * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[宇都宮駅]]西口から ** [[関東自動車 (栃木県)|関東バス]] 戸祭台循環線に乗車約15分、「第二グリーンヒル北」下車徒歩10分。
;車 * [[国道119号]]（[[宇都宮環状道路|宮環]]）長岡町交差点を南進1分、もしくは同（[[日光街道]]）の上戸祭町交差点を東進1分の[[栃木県道157号下岡本上戸祭線|長岡街道]]から枝道を東進1分。
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧]] * [[北山古墳群]] * [[瓦塚古墳群]] * [[谷口山古墳]] * [[長岡百穴古墳]] * [[豊郷まほろばの道]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:とまつりおおつかこふん}} [[Category:栃木県の古墳]] [[Category:宇都宮市]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

{{日本の古墳| 名称=大塚古墳| 所在地=栃木県宇都宮市上戸祭町| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|35|27|N|139|52|26|E|scale:20000}}| 形状=円墳| 規模=全長53.4m、高さ6.2m| 築造年代=6世紀後半| 被葬者=| 史跡指定=県指定史跡（大塚古墳）| 出土品=| 画像=}}
'''戸祭大塚古墳'''（とまつりおおつかこふん）は、[[栃木県]][[宇都宮市]]上戸祭町に存在する[[円墳]]である。通常は単に'''大塚古墳'''と呼ぶ。
== 概要 == 大塚古墳は、径53.4メートル、高さ6.2メートルの2段築成の大型円墳である。1段目円墳の高さは3.4メートルで、この上に高さ2.8メートルの2段目円墳が乗っている。2段目円墳の頂上には径9メートルの平坦部があり、載頭円錐型の全国的にも珍しい美しい円墳として知られる。墳の周縁部には一部破損しているが北西側から東側にかけて幅約6.6メートル、深さ約1.3メートルほどの周溝が現存する。石室が盗掘され詳細は不明だが、玄室長5.17メートル、凝灰岩割石の平積み横穴式石室と推定されており、築造年代は[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]後半と考えられている。
[[1956年]]（[[昭和]]32年）[[6月30日]]、同じく市内の[[笹塚古墳]]とともに栃木県指定史跡に登録されている。
== 周辺環境 == 大塚古墳は宇都宮市北部の宇都宮丘陵の南部、[[戸祭山]]の北側の[[田川 (利根川水系)|田川]]西岸に立地する。周辺には当墳をはじめ、[[谷口山古墳]]や[[北山古墳群]]、[[瓦塚古墳群]]、[[長岡百穴古墳]]などの古代古墳群、また[[奈良時代]]の遺跡である[[水道山瓦窯跡]]などの古代遺跡が数多く認められており、[[豊郷まほろばの道]]が整備されている。
== アクセス == ;路線バス * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[宇都宮駅]]西口から ** [[関東自動車 (栃木県)|関東バス]] 戸祭台循環線に乗車約15分、「第二グリーンヒル北」下車徒歩10分。
;車 * [[国道119号]]（[[宇都宮環状道路|宮環]]）長岡町交差点を南進1分、もしくは同（[[日光街道]]）の上戸祭町交差点を東進1分の[[栃木県道157号下岡本上戸祭線|長岡街道]]から枝道を東進1分。
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧]] * [[栃木県指定文化財一覧]] * [[北山古墳群]] * [[瓦塚古墳群]] * [[谷口山古墳]] * [[長岡百穴古墳]] * [[豊郷まほろばの道]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:とまつりおおつかこふん}} [[Category:栃木県の古墳]] [[Category:宇都宮市の歴史]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:栃木県指定史跡]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称=大塚古墳 |画像=[[ファイル:Tomatsuri Otsuka Kofun.jpg|250px]] |所在地=[[栃木県]][[宇都宮市]][[上戸祭町]]2982-1{{sfn|塙|2008|p=106}} |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|35|27|N|139|52|26|E|scale:20000}} |形状=[[円墳]]{{sfn|塙|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|宇都宮市 編|1992|p=55}} |規模=全長53.4[[メートル|m]]、高さ6.2m{{sfn|塙|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|宇都宮市 編|1992|p=55}} |築造年代=[[6世紀]]後半{{sfn|塙|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|宇都宮市 編|1992|p=55}} |被葬者= |出土品= |史跡指定=県指定史跡（大塚古墳） }} '''戸祭大塚古墳'''（とまつりおおつかこふん）は、[[栃木県]][[宇都宮市]][[上戸祭町]]に存在する[[円墳]]である{{sfn|塙|2008|p=106}}。通常は単に'''大塚古墳'''と呼ぶ{{sfn|塙|2008|p=106}}。
== 概要 == 大塚古墳は、直径53.4[[メートル]]、高さ6.2メートルの2段築成の大型円墳である{{sfn|塙|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|宇都宮市 編|1992|p=55}}。1段目円墳の高さは3.4メートルで、この上に高さ2.8メートルの2段目円墳が乗っている{{sfn|宇都宮市 編|1992|p=55}}。2段目円墳の頂上には径9メートルの平坦部があり{{sfn|宇都宮市 編|1992|p=55}}、載頭円錐型の全国的にも珍しい美しい円墳として知られる。墳の周縁部には北西側から東側にかけて幅約6.6メートル、深さ約1.3メートルほどの周溝が現存する{{sfn|塙|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|宇都宮市 編|1992|p=55}}。[[1965年]]（昭和40年）に[[石室]]が[[盗掘]]され{{sfn|塙|2008|p=106}}詳細は不明だが、[[玄室]]は長さ5.17メートル、幅1.8 - 2.1メートル、高さ1.75 - 2.14メートル、[[凝灰岩]]割石の平積み[[横穴式石室]]と推定されており、築造年代は[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]後半と考えられている{{sfn|宇都宮市 編|1992|p=55}}。追葬が行われた形跡がある{{sfn|宇都宮市 編|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|塙|2008|p=107}}。[[副葬品]]に関する記録は残っていない{{sfn|宇都宮市 編|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|塙|2008|p=106}}。盗掘を受けた緊急調査であったため{{sfn|塙|2008|p=107}}、埋まっている[[羨道]]は調査されていない{{sfn|宇都宮市 編|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|塙|2008|p=107}}。
[[1956年]]（[[昭和]]32年）[[6月30日]]、同じく市内の[[笹塚古墳]]とともに栃木県指定史跡に指定されている。
== 周辺環境 == [[ファイル:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|オオジノ古墳群]] 大塚古墳は宇都宮市北部の[[宇都宮丘陵]]の南部{{sfn|宇都宮市 編|1992|p=55}}、[[戸祭山]]の北側の[[田川 (利根川水系)|田川]]西岸に立地する。周辺には当墳をはじめ、[[谷口山古墳]]や[[北山古墳群]]、[[瓦塚古墳群]]、[[長岡百穴古墳]]などの古代古墳群、また[[奈良時代]]の遺跡である[[水道山瓦窯跡]]などの古代遺跡が数多く認められており、[[豊郷まほろばの道]]が整備されている。
大塚古墳のすぐ北側の山中には数基の円墳から成るオオジノ古墳群がある{{sfn|塙|2008|p=107}}。
== アクセス == '''路線バス''' * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[宇都宮駅]]宇都宮駅の[[バス乗り場|西口]]から[[関東自動車 (栃木県)|関東バス]]利用 ** 戸祭台循環線に乗車約15分、「第二グリーンヒル北」下車徒歩10分。 ** [[日光市|日光]]方面行きに乗車、「長岡街道入口」下車、長岡街道経由、約1[[キロメートル]]{{sfn|塙|2008|p=106}}。
'''車''' * [[国道119号]]（[[宇都宮環状道路|宮環]]）長岡町交差点を南進1分、もしくは同（[[日光街道]]）の上戸祭町交差点を東進1分の[[栃木県道157号下岡本上戸祭線|長岡街道]]から枝道を東進1分。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * {{cite book|和書|author=[[塙静夫]]|title=うつのみや歴史探訪 史跡案内九十九景|date=2008-09-27|publisher=随想舎|page=287|isbn=978-4-88748-179-4|ref={{sfnref|塙|2008}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=宇都宮市 編|title=改訂 うつのみやの歴史|publisher=宇都宮市|date=1992-03-31|page=418|ncid=BN07977757|ref={{sfnref|宇都宮市 編|1992}}}}
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧]] * [[栃木県指定文化財一覧]] * [[北山古墳群]] * [[瓦塚古墳群]] * [[谷口山古墳]] * [[長岡百穴古墳]] * [[豊郷まほろばの道]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.ueis.ed.jp/kyouzai/mahoroba16/html/otsuka/otsuka2.html 大塚古墳（くわしく）] - 宇都宮市教育センター
{{デフォルトソート:とまつりおおつかこふん}} [[カテゴリ:栃木県の古墳]] [[カテゴリ:宇都宮市の歴史]] [[カテゴリ:円墳]] [[カテゴリ:栃木県指定史跡]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Tomatsuri Otsuka Kofun.jpg|250px]] | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Kamitomatsuri-chō 2982-1{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}} | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|27|N|139|52|26|E|scale:20000}} | Shape = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 53.4 m, Height: 6.2 m{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Construction Era = Late 6th century{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Ōtsuka Kofun) }}
'''Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (戸祭大塚古墳) is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳}} located in Kamitomatsuri-chō, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. It is commonly referred to simply as '''Ōtsuka Kofun'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
== Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a large, two-tiered round tumulus with a diameter of 53.4 meters and a height of 6.2 meters{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.
In 1965 (Showa 40), the burial chamber was looted{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. While the details remain unclear, the chamber is believed to have been a horizontal stone chamber made of tuff slabs. The estimated dimensions are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial artifacts remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was designated a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳}}.
== Surrounding Environment == [[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]] Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}}. There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.
To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes. ** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
'''By Car''' * From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}): ** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|author=Hanawa Shizuo|title=Utsunomiya Rekishi Tanbō: Shiseki Annai Kyūjūkyūkei|publisher=Zuisōsha|date=2008-09-27|isbn=978-4-88748-179-4|ref={{sfnref|Hanawa|2008}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Utsunomiya City|title=Revised History of Utsunomiya|publisher=Utsunomiya City|date=1992-03-31|ncid=BN07977757|ref={{sfnref|Utsunomiya City Board|1992}}}}

The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.
In 1965 (Showa 40), the burial chamber was looted{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. While the details remain unclear, the chamber is believed to have been a horizontal stone chamber made of tuff slabs. The estimated dimensions are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial artifacts remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was designated a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳}}.
== Surrounding Environment == [[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]] Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}}. There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.
To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes. ** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
'''By Car''' * From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}): ** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|author=Hanawa Shizuo|title=Utsunomiya Rekishi Tanbō: Shiseki Annai Kyūjūkyūkei|publisher=Zuisōsha|date=2008-09-27|isbn=978-4-88748-179-4|ref={{sfnref|Hanawa|2008}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Utsunomiya City|title=Revised History of Utsunomiya|publisher=Utsunomiya City|date=1992-03-31|ncid=BN07977757|ref={{sfnref|Utsunomiya City Board|1992}}}}

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Tomatsuri Otsuka Kofun.jpg|250px]] | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Kamitomatsuri-chō 2982-1{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}} | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|27|N|139|52|26|E|scale:20000}} | Shape = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 53.4 m, Height: 6.2 m{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Construction Era = Late 6th century{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Ōtsuka Kofun) }}
'''Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (戸祭大塚古墳) is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳}} located in Kamitomatsuri-chō, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. It is commonly referred to simply as '''Ōtsuka Kofun'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
== Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a large, two-tiered round tumulus with a diameter of 53.4 meters and a height of 6.2 meters{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.
In 1965 (Showa 40), the burial chamber was looted{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. While the details remain unclear, the chamber is believed to have been a horizontal stone chamber made of tuff slabs. The estimated dimensions are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial artifacts remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was designated a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳}}.
== Surrounding Environment == [[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]] Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}}. There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.
To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes. ** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
'''By Car''' * From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}): ** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Tomatsuri Otsuka Kofun.jpg|250px]] | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Kamitomatsuri-chō 2982-1{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}} | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|27|N|139|52|26|E|scale:20000}} | Shape = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 53.4 m, Height: 6.2 m{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Construction Era = Late 6th century{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Ōtsuka Kofun) }}
'''Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (戸祭大塚古墳) is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳}} located in Kamitomatsuri-chō, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. It is commonly referred to simply as '''Ōtsuka Kofun'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
== Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a large, two-tiered round tumulus with a diameter of 53.4 meters and a height of 6.2 meters{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.
In 1965 (Showa 40), the burial chamber was looted{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. While the details remain unclear, the chamber is believed to have been a horizontal stone chamber made of tuff slabs. The estimated dimensions are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial artifacts remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was designated a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳}}.
== Surrounding Environment == [[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]] Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}}. There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.
To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes. ** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
'''By Car''' * From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}): ** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.

Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}}. There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.
To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes. ** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
'''By Car''' * From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}): ** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|author=Hanawa Shizuo|title=Utsunomiya Rekishi Tanbō: Shiseki Annai Kyūjūkyūkei|publisher=Zuisōsha|date=2008-09-27|isbn=978-4-88748-179-4|ref={{sfnref|Hanawa|2008}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Utsunomiya City|title=Revised History of Utsunomiya|publisher=Utsunomiya City|date=1992-03-31|ncid=BN07977757|ref={{sfnref|Utsunomiya City Board|1992}}}}

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Tomatsuri Otsuka Kofun.jpg|250px]] | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Kamitomatsuri-chō 2982-1{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}} | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|27|N|139|52|26|E|scale:20000}} | Shape = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 53.4 m, Height: 6.2 m{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Construction Era = Late 6th century{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Ōtsuka Kofun) }}
'''Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (戸祭大塚古墳) is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳}} located in Kamitomatsuri-chō, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. It is commonly referred to simply as '''Ōtsuka Kofun'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
== Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a large, two-tiered round tumulus with a diameter of 53.4 meters and a height of 6.2 meters{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.
In 1965 (Showa 40), the burial chamber was looted{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. While the details remain unclear, the chamber is believed to have been a horizontal stone chamber made of tuff slabs. The estimated dimensions are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial artifacts remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was designated a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳}}.
== Surrounding Environment == [[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]] Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}}. There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.
To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes. ** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
'''By Car''' * From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}): ** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Tomatsuri Otsuka Kofun.jpg|250px]] | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Kamitomatsuri-chō 2982-1{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}} | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|27|N|139|52|26|E|scale:20000}} | Shape = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 53.4 m, Height: 6.2 m{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Construction Era = Late 6th century{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Ōtsuka Kofun) }}
'''Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (戸祭大塚古墳) is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳}} located in Kamitomatsuri-chō, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. It is commonly referred to simply as '''Ōtsuka Kofun'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
== Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a large, two-tiered round tumulus with a diameter of 53.4 meters and a height of 6.2 meters{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.
In 1965 (Showa 40), the burial chamber was looted{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. While the details remain unclear, the chamber is believed to have been a horizontal stone chamber made of tuff slabs. The estimated dimensions are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial artifacts remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was designated a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳}}.
== Surrounding Environment == [[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]] Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}}. There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.
To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes. ** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
'''By Car''' * From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}): ** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|author=Hanawa Shizuo|title=Utsunomiya Rekishi Tanbō: Shiseki Annai Kyūjūkyūkei|publisher=Zuisōsha|date=2008-09-27|isbn=978-4-88748-179-4|ref={{sfnref|Hanawa|2008}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Utsunomiya City|title=Revised History of Utsunomiya|publisher=Utsunomiya City|date=1992-03-31|ncid=BN07977757|ref={{sfnref|Utsunomiya City Board|1992}}}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Tochigi Prefecture-designated cultural properties|ja|栃木県指定文化財一覧}} * {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}} * {{ill|Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}} * {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}} * {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} * {{ill|Shimo-Kuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|下栗大塚古墳}}
== External Links == * [http://www.ueis.ed.jp/kyouzai/mahoroba16/html/otsuka/otsuka2.html Detailed Information on Ōtsuka Kofun] - Utsunomiya City Education Center

The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. == Surrounding Environment == [[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]] Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}}. There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.
To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes. ** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
'''By Car''' * From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}): ** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Tomatsuri Otsuka Kofun.jpg|250px]] | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Kamitomatsuri-chō 2982-1{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}} | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|27|N|139|52|26|E|scale:20000}} | Shape = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 53.4 m, Height: 6.2 m{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Construction Era = Late 6th century{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Ōtsuka Kofun) }}
'''Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (戸祭大塚古墳) is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳}} located in Kamitomatsuri-chō, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. It is commonly referred to simply as '''Ōtsuka Kofun'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
== Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a large, two-tiered round tumulus with a diameter of 53.4 meters and a height of 6.2 meters{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.
In 1965 (Showa 40), the burial chamber was looted{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. While the details remain unclear, the chamber is believed to have been a horizontal stone chamber made of tuff slabs. The estimated dimensions are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial artifacts remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was designated a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳}}.
== Surrounding Environment == [[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]] Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}}. There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.
To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes. ** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
'''By Car''' * From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}): ** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Tomatsuri Otsuka Kofun.jpg|250px]] | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Kamitomatsuri-chō 2982-1{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}} | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|27|N|139|52|26|E|scale:20000}} | Shape = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 53.4 m, Height: 6.2 m{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Construction Era = Late 6th century{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Ōtsuka Kofun) }}
'''Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (戸祭大塚古墳) is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳}} located in Kamitomatsuri-chō, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. It is commonly referred to simply as '''Ōtsuka Kofun'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
== Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a large, two-tiered round tumulus with a diameter of 53.4 meters and a height of 6.2 meters{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.
In 1965 (Showa 40), the burial chamber was looted{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. While the details remain unclear, the chamber is believed to have been a horizontal stone chamber made of tuff slabs. The estimated dimensions are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial artifacts remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was designated a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳}}.
== Surrounding Environment == [[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]] Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}}. There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.
To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes. ** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
'''By Car''' * From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}): ** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.

== Surrounding Environment == [[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]] Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}}. There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.
To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes. ** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
'''By Car''' * From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}): ** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.

[[Category:kk]] [[Category:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]] [[ja:戸祭大塚古墳]] [[Category:new pages]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Tomatsuri Otsuka Kofun.jpg|250px]] | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Kamitomatsuri-chō 2982-1{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}} | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|27|N|139|52|26|E|scale:20000}} | Shape = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 53.4 m, Height: 6.2 m{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Construction Era = Late 6th century{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Ōtsuka Kofun) }}
'''Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (戸祭大塚古墳) is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳}} located in Kamitomatsuri-chō, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. It is commonly referred to simply as '''Ōtsuka Kofun'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
== Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a large, two-tiered round tumulus with a diameter of 53.4 meters and a height of 6.2 meters{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.
In 1965 (Showa 40), the burial chamber was looted{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. While the details remain unclear, the chamber is believed to have been a horizontal stone chamber made of tuff slabs. The estimated dimensions are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial artifacts remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was designated a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳}}.
== Surrounding Environment == [[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]] Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}}. There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.
To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes. ** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
'''By Car''' * From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}): ** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Tomatsuri Otsuka Kofun.jpg|250px]] | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Kamitomatsuri-chō 2982-1{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}} | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|27|N|139|52|26|E|scale:20000}} | Shape = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 53.4 m, Height: 6.2 m{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Construction Era = Late 6th century{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Ōtsuka Kofun) }}
'''Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (戸祭大塚古墳) is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳}} located in Kamitomatsuri-chō, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. It is commonly referred to simply as '''Ōtsuka Kofun'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
== Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a large, two-tiered round tumulus with a diameter of 53.4 meters and a height of 6.2 meters{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.
In 1965 (Showa 40), the burial chamber was looted{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. While the details remain unclear, the chamber is believed to have been a horizontal stone chamber made of tuff slabs. The estimated dimensions are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial artifacts remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was designated a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳}}.
== Surrounding Environment == [[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]] Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}}. There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.
To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes. ** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
'''By Car''' * From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}): ** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Tomatsuri Otsuka Kofun.jpg|250px]] | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Kamitomatsuri-chō 2982-1{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}} | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|27|N|139|52|26|E|scale:20000}} | Shape = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 53.4 m, Height: 6.2 m{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Construction Era = Late 6th century{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Ōtsuka Kofun) }}
'''Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (戸祭大塚古墳) is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳}} located in Kamitomatsuri-chō, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. It is commonly referred to simply as '''Ōtsuka Kofun'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
== Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a large, two-tiered round tumulus with a diameter of 53.4 meters and a height of 6.2 meters{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.
In 1965 (Showa 40), the burial chamber was looted{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. While the details remain unclear, the chamber is believed to have been a horizontal stone chamber made of tuff slabs. The estimated dimensions are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial artifacts remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was designated a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳}}.
== Surrounding Environment == [[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]] Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}}. There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.
To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes. ** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
'''By Car''' * From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}): ** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.

'''Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (戸祭大塚古墳) is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳}} located in Kamitomatsuri-chō, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. It is commonly referred to simply as '''Ōtsuka Kofun'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
== Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a large, two-tiered round tumulus with a diameter of 53.4 meters and a height of 6.2 meters{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.
In 1965 (Showa 40), the burial chamber was looted{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. While the details remain unclear, the chamber is believed to have been a horizontal stone chamber made of tuff slabs. The estimated dimensions are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial artifacts remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was designated a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳}}.
== Surrounding Environment == [[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]] Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}}. There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.
To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes. ** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
'''By Car''' * From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}): ** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.

A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.
In 1965 (Showa 40), the burial chamber was looted{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. While the details remain unclear, the chamber is believed to have been a horizontal stone chamber made of tuff slabs. The estimated dimensions are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial artifacts remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was designated a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳}}.
== Surrounding Environment == [[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]] Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}}. There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.
To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes. ** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
'''By Car''' * From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}): ** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Tomatsuri Otsuka Kofun.jpg|250px]] | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Kamitomatsuri-chō 2982-1{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}} | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|27|N|139|52|26|E|scale:20000}} | Shape = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 53.4 m, Height: 6.2 m{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Construction Era = Late 6th century{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Ōtsuka Kofun) }}
'''Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (戸祭大塚古墳) is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳}} located in Kamitomatsuri-chō, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. It is commonly referred to simply as '''Ōtsuka Kofun'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
== Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a large, two-tiered round tumulus with a diameter of 53.4 meters and a height of 6.2 meters{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.
In 1965 (Showa 40), the burial chamber was looted{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. While the details remain unclear, the chamber is believed to have been a horizontal stone chamber made of tuff slabs. The estimated dimensions are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial artifacts remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was designated a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳}}.
== Surrounding Environment == [[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]] Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}}. There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.
To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes. ** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
'''By Car''' * From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}): ** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.

The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.
To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes. ** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
'''By Car''' * From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}): ** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.

[[Category:kk]] [[Category:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]] [[ja:戸祭大塚古墳]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:11]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Tomatsuri Otsuka Kofun.jpg|250px]] | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Kamitomatsuri-chō 2982-1{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}} | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|27|N|139|52|26|E|scale:20000}} | Shape = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 53.4 m, Height: 6.2 m{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Construction Era = Late 6th century{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Ōtsuka Kofun) }}
'''Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (戸祭大塚古墳) is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳}} located in Kamitomatsuri-chō, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. It is commonly referred to simply as '''Ōtsuka Kofun'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
== Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a large, two-tiered round tumulus with a diameter of 53.4 meters and a height of 6.2 meters{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.
In 1965 (Showa 40), the burial chamber was looted{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. While the details remain unclear, the chamber is believed to have been a horizontal stone chamber made of tuff slabs. The estimated dimensions are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial artifacts remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was designated a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳}}.
== Surrounding Environment == [[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]] Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}}. There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.
To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes. ** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
'''By Car''' * From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}): ** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Tomatsuri Otsuka Kofun.jpg|250px]] | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Kamitomatsuri-chō 2982-1{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}} | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|27|N|139|52|26|E|scale:20000}} | Shape = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 53.4 m, Height: 6.2 m{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Construction Era = Late 6th century{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Ōtsuka Kofun) }}
'''Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (戸祭大塚古墳) is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳}} located in Kamitomatsuri-chō, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. It is commonly referred to simply as '''Ōtsuka Kofun'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
== Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a large, two-tiered round tumulus with a diameter of 53.4 meters and a height of 6.2 meters{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.
In 1965 (Showa 40), the burial chamber was looted{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. While the details remain unclear, the chamber is believed to have been a horizontal stone chamber made of tuff slabs. The estimated dimensions are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial artifacts remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was designated a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳}}.
== Surrounding Environment == [[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]] Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}}. There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.
To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes. ** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
'''By Car''' * From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}): ** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|author=Hanawa Shizuo|title=Utsunomiya Rekishi Tanbō: Shiseki Annai Kyūjūkyūkei|publisher=Zuisōsha|date=2008-09-27|isbn=978-4-88748-179-4|ref={{sfnref|Hanawa|2008}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Utsunomiya City|title=Revised History of Utsunomiya|publisher=Utsunomiya City|date=1992-03-31|ncid=BN07977757|ref={{sfnref|Utsunomiya City Board|1992}}}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Tochigi Prefecture-designated cultural properties|ja|栃木県指定文化財一覧}} * {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}} * {{ill|Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}} * {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}} * {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} * {{ill|Shimo-Kuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|下栗大塚古墳}}
== External Links == * [http://www.ueis.ed.jp/kyouzai/mahoroba16/html/otsuka/otsuka2.html Detailed Information on Ōtsuka Kofun] - Utsunomiya City Education Center
