[[Category:kk]] [[Category:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]] [[ja:戸祭大塚古墳]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] [[Category:円墳]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:円墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Tomatsuri Otsuka Kofun.jpg|250px]] | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Kamitomatsuri-chō 2982-1{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}} | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|27|N|139|52|26|E|scale:20000}} | Shape = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 53.4 m, Height: 6.2 m{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Construction Era = Late 6th century{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Ōtsuka Kofun) }}
'''Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (戸祭大塚古墳) is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳}} located in Kamitomatsuri-chō, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. It is commonly referred to simply as '''Ōtsuka Kofun'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
== Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a large, two-tiered round tumulus with a diameter of 53.4 meters and a height of 6.2 meters{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.
In 1965 (Showa 40), the burial chamber was looted{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. While the details remain unclear, the chamber is believed to have been a horizontal stone chamber made of tuff slabs. The estimated dimensions are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial artifacts remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was designated a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳}}.
== Surrounding Environment == [[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]] Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}}. There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.
To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes. ** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
'''By Car''' * From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}): ** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.

'''Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (戸祭大塚古墳) is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳}} located in Kamitomatsuri-chō, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. It is commonly referred to simply as '''Ōtsuka Kofun'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.

The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.

The estimated dimensions are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial artifacts remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was designated a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳}}.
== Surrounding Environment == [[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]] Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}}. There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.
To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.

Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes. ** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.

[[Category:kk]] [[Category:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]] [[ja:戸祭大塚古墳]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] [[Category:円墳]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:円墳]] [[Category:円墳]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:円墳]] [[Category:円墳]][[sn:Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:円墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Tomatsuri Otsuka Kofun.jpg|250px]] | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Kamitomatsuri-chō 2982-1{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}} | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|27|N|139|52|26|E|scale:20000}} | Shape = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 53.4 m, Height: 6.2 m{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Construction Era = Late 6th century{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Ōtsuka Kofun) }}
'''Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (戸祭大塚古墳) is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳}} located in Kamitomatsuri-chō, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. It is commonly referred to simply as '''Ōtsuka Kofun'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
== Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a large, two-tiered round tumulus with a diameter of 53.4 meters and a height of 6.2 meters{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.
In 1965 (Showa 40), the burial chamber was looted{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. While the details remain unclear, the chamber is believed to have been a horizontal stone chamber made of tuff slabs. The estimated dimensions are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial artifacts remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was designated a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳}}.
== Surrounding Environment == [[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]] Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}}. There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.
To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes. ** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
'''By Car''' * From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}): ** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Tomatsuri Otsuka Kofun.jpg|250px]] | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Kamitomatsuri-chō 2982-1{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}} | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|27|N|139|52|26|E|scale:20000}} | Shape = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 53.4 m, Height: 6.2 m{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Construction Era = Late 6th century{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Ōtsuka Kofun) }}
'''Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (戸祭大塚古墳) is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳}} located in Kamitomatsuri-chō, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. It is commonly referred to simply as '''Ōtsuka Kofun'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
== Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a large, two-tiered round tumulus with a diameter of 53.4 meters and a height of 6.2 meters{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.
In 1965 (Showa 40), the burial chamber was looted{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. While the details remain unclear, the chamber is believed to have been a horizontal stone chamber made of tuff slabs. The estimated dimensions are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial artifacts remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was designated a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳}}.
== Surrounding Environment == [[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]] Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}}. There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.
To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes. ** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
'''By Car''' * From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}): ** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Tomatsuri Otsuka Kofun.jpg|250px]] | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Kamitomatsuri-chō 2982-1{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}} | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|27|N|139|52|26|E|scale:20000}} | Shape = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 53.4 m, Height: 6.2 m{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Construction Era = Late 6th century{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Ōtsuka Kofun) }}
'''Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (戸祭大塚古墳) is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳}} located in Kamitomatsuri-chō, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. It is commonly referred to simply as '''Ōtsuka Kofun'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
== Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a large, two-tiered round tumulus with a diameter of 53.4 meters and a height of 6.2 meters{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.
In 1965 (Showa 40), the burial chamber was looted{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. While the details remain unclear, the chamber is believed to have been a horizontal stone chamber made of tuff slabs. The estimated dimensions are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial artifacts remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was designated a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳}}.
== Surrounding Environment == [[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]] Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}}. There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.
To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes. ** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
'''By Car''' * From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}): ** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.

'''Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (戸祭大塚古墳) is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳}} located in Kamitomatsuri-chō, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. It is commonly referred to simply as '''Ōtsuka Kofun'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.

The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.
In 1965 (Showa 40), the burial chamber was looted{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. While the details remain unclear, the chamber is believed to have been a horizontal stone chamber made of tuff slabs. The estimated dimensions are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial artifacts remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was designated a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳}}.
== Surrounding Environment == [[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]] Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}}. There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.
To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.

Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes. ** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Tomatsuri Otsuka Kofun.jpg|250px]] | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Kamitomatsuri-chō 2982-1{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}} | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|27|N|139|52|26|E|scale:20000}} | Shape = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 53.4 m, Height: 6.2 m{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Construction Era = Late 6th century{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Ōtsuka Kofun) }}
'''Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (戸祭大塚古墳) is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳}} located in Kamitomatsuri-chō, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. It is commonly referred to simply as '''Ōtsuka Kofun'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
== Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a large, two-tiered round tumulus with a diameter of 53.4 meters and a height of 6.2 meters{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.
In 1965 (Showa 40), the burial chamber was looted{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. While the details remain unclear, the chamber is believed to have been a horizontal stone chamber made of tuff slabs. The estimated dimensions are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial artifacts remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was designated a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳}}.
== Surrounding Environment == [[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]] Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}}. There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.
To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes. ** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
'''By Car''' * From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}): ** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Tomatsuri Otsuka Kofun.jpg|250px]] | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Kamitomatsuri-chō 2982-1{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}} | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|27|N|139|52|26|E|scale:20000}} | Shape = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 53.4 m, Height: 6.2 m{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Construction Era = Late 6th century{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Ōtsuka Kofun) }}
'''Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (戸祭大塚古墳) is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳}} located in Kamitomatsuri-chō, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. It is commonly referred to simply as '''Ōtsuka Kofun'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
== Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a large, two-tiered round tumulus with a diameter of 53.4 meters and a height of 6.2 meters{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.
In 1965 (Showa 40), the burial chamber was looted{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. While the details remain unclear, the chamber is believed to have been a horizontal stone chamber made of tuff slabs. The estimated dimensions are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial artifacts remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was designated a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳}}.
== Surrounding Environment == [[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]] Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}}. There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.
To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes. ** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
'''By Car''' * From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}): ** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Tomatsuri Otsuka Kofun.jpg|250px]] | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Kamitomatsuri-chō 2982-1{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}} | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|27|N|139|52|26|E|scale:20000}} | Shape = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 53.4 m, Height: 6.2 m{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Construction Era = Late 6th century{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Ōtsuka Kofun) }}
'''Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (戸祭大塚古墳) is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳}} located in Kamitomatsuri-chō, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. It is commonly referred to simply as '''Ōtsuka Kofun'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
== Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a large, two-tiered round tumulus with a diameter of 53.4 meters and a height of 6.2 meters{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.
In 1965 (Showa 40), the burial chamber was looted{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. While the details remain unclear, the chamber is believed to have been a horizontal stone chamber made of tuff slabs. The estimated dimensions are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial artifacts remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was designated a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳}}.
== Surrounding Environment == [[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]] Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}}. There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.
To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes. ** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
'''By Car''' * From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}): ** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.

The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Tomatsuri Otsuka Kofun.jpg|250px]] | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Kamitomatsuri-chō 2982-1{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}} | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|27|N|139|52|26|E|scale:20000}} | Shape = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 53.4 m, Height: 6.2 m{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Construction Era = Late 6th century{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Ōtsuka Kofun) }}
'''Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (戸祭大塚古墳) is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳}} located in Kamitomatsuri-chō, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. It is commonly referred to simply as '''Ōtsuka Kofun'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
== Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a large, two-tiered round tumulus with a diameter of 53.4 meters and a height of 6.2 meters{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.
In 1965 (Showa 40), the burial chamber was looted{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. While the details remain unclear, the chamber is believed to have been a horizontal stone chamber made of tuff slabs. The estimated dimensions are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial artifacts remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was designated a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳}}.
== Surrounding Environment == [[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]] Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}}. There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.
To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes. ** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
'''By Car''' * From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}): ** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.

On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was designated a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳}}.
== Surrounding Environment == [[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]] Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}}. There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.
To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes. ** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
'''By Car''' * From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}): ** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Tomatsuri Otsuka Kofun.jpg|250px]] | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Kamitomatsuri-chō 2982-1{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}} | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|27|N|139|52|26|E|scale:20000}} | Shape = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 53.4 m, Height: 6.2 m{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Construction Era = Late 6th century{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Ōtsuka Kofun) }}
'''Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (戸祭大塚古墳) is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳}} located in Kamitomatsuri-chō, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. It is commonly referred to simply as '''Ōtsuka Kofun'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
== Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a large, two-tiered round tumulus with a diameter of 53.4 meters and a height of 6.2 meters{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.
In 1965 (Showa 40), the burial chamber was looted{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. While the details remain unclear, the chamber is believed to have been a horizontal stone chamber made of tuff slabs. The estimated dimensions are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial artifacts remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was designated a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳}}.
== Surrounding Environment == [[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]] Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}}. There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.
To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes. ** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
'''By Car''' * From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}): ** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.

'''Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (戸祭大塚古墳) is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} located in Kamitomatsuri-chō, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. It is commonly referred to simply as '''Ōtsuka Kofun'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
== Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a large, two-tiered round tumulus with a diameter of 53.4 meters and a height of 6.2 meters{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.
In 1965 (Showa 40), the burial chamber was looted{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. While the details remain unclear, the chamber is believed to have been a horizontal stone chamber made of tuff slabs. The estimated dimensions are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial artifacts remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was designated a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳}}.
== Surrounding Environment == [[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]] Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}}. There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.
To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes. ** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
'''By Car''' * From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}): ** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.

The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.

The estimated dimensions are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial artifacts remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was designated a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳}}.

There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.
To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.

Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes. ** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Tomatsuri Otsuka Kofun.jpg|250px]] | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Kamitomatsuri-chō 2982-1{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}} | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|27|N|139|52|26|E|scale:20000}} | Shape = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 53.4 m, Height: 6.2 m{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Construction Era = Late 6th century{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Ōtsuka Kofun) }}
'''Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (戸祭大塚古墳) is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} located in Kamitomatsuri-chō, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. It is commonly referred to simply as '''Ōtsuka Kofun'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
== Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a large, two-tiered round tumulus with a diameter of 53.4 meters and a height of 6.2 meters{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.
In 1965 (Showa 40), the burial chamber was looted{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. While the details remain unclear, the chamber is believed to have been a horizontal stone chamber made of tuff slabs. The estimated dimensions are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial artifacts remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was designated a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳}}.
== Surrounding Environment == [[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]] Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}}. There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.
To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes. ** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
'''By Car''' * From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}): ** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.

The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. {{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Tomatsuri Otsuka Kofun.jpg|250px]] | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Kamitomatsuri-chō 2982-1{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}} | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|27|N|139|52|26|E|scale:20000}} | Shape = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 53.4 m, Height: 6.2 m{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Construction Era = Late 6th century{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Ōtsuka Kofun) }}
'''Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (戸祭大塚古墳) is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} located in Kamitomatsuri-chō, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. It is commonly referred to simply as '''Ōtsuka Kofun'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
== Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a large, two-tiered round tumulus with a diameter of 53.4 meters and a height of 6.2 meters{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.
In 1965 (Showa 40), the burial chamber was looted{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. While the details remain unclear, the chamber is believed to have been a horizontal stone chamber made of tuff slabs. The estimated dimensions are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial artifacts remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was designated a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳}}.
== Surrounding Environment == [[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]] Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}}. There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.
To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes. ** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
'''By Car''' * From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}): ** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.

The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Tomatsuri Otsuka Kofun.jpg|250px]] | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Kamitomatsuri-chō 2982-1{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}} | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|27|N|139|52|26|E|scale:20000}} | Shape = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 53.4 m, Height: 6.2 m{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Construction Era = Late 6th century{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Ōtsuka Kofun) }}
'''Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (戸祭大塚古墳) is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} located in Kamitomatsuri-chō, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. It is commonly referred to simply as '''Ōtsuka Kofun'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
== Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a large, two-tiered round tumulus with a diameter of 53.4 meters and a height of 6.2 meters{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.
In 1965 (Showa 40), the burial chamber was looted{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. While the details remain unclear, the chamber is believed to have been a horizontal stone chamber made of tuff slabs. The estimated dimensions are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial artifacts remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was designated a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳}}.
== Surrounding Environment == [[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]] Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}}. There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.
To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes. ** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
'''By Car''' * From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}): ** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.

The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.

It is thought to date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial artifacts remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was designated a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳}}.
== Surrounding Environment == [[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]] Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}}. There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.
To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes. ** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
'''By Car''' * From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}): ** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Tomatsuri Otsuka Kofun.jpg|250px]] | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Kamitomatsuri-chō 2982-1{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}} | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|27|N|139|52|26|E|scale:20000}} | Shape = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 53.4 m, Height: 6.2 m{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Construction Era = Late 6th century{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Ōtsuka Kofun) }}
'''Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (戸祭大塚古墳) is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} located in Kamitomatsuri-chō, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. It is commonly referred to simply as '''Ōtsuka Kofun'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
== Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a large, two-tiered round tumulus with a diameter of 53.4 meters and a height of 6.2 meters{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.
In 1965 (Showa 40), the burial chamber was looted{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. While the details remain unclear, the chamber is believed to have been a horizontal stone chamber made of tuff slabs. The estimated dimensions are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial artifacts remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was designated a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳}}.
== Surrounding Environment == [[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]] Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}}. There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.
To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes. ** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
'''By Car''' * From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}): ** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Tomatsuri Otsuka Kofun.jpg|250px]] | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Kamitomatsuri-chō 2982-1{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}} | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|27|N|139|52|26|E|scale:20000}} | Shape = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 53.4 m, Height: 6.2 m{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Construction Era = Late 6th century{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Ōtsuka Kofun) }}
'''Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (戸祭大塚古墳) is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} located in Kamitomatsuri-chō, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. It is commonly referred to simply as '''Ōtsuka Kofun'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
== Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a large, two-tiered round tumulus with a diameter of 53.4 meters and a height of 6.2 meters{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.
In 1965 (Showa 40), the burial chamber was looted{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. While the details remain unclear, the chamber is believed to have been a horizontal stone chamber made of tuff slabs. The estimated dimensions are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial artifacts remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was designated a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳}}.
== Surrounding Environment == [[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]] Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}}. There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.
To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes. ** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
'''By Car''' * From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}): ** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.
