'''Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (戸祭大塚古墳) is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} located in Kamitomatsuri-chō, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. It is commonly referred to simply as '''Ōtsuka Kofun'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
== Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a large, two-tiered round tumulus with a diameter of 53.4 meters and a height of 6.2 meters{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.
In 1965 (Showa 40), the burial chamber was looted{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. While the details remain unclear, the chamber is believed to have been a horizontal stone chamber made of tuff slabs. The estimated dimensions are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial artifacts remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was designated a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳}}.
== Surrounding Environment == [[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]] Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves}}. There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.
To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes. ** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
'''By Car''' * From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}): ** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.

'''Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (戸祭大塚古墳) is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} located in Kamitomatsuri-chō, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. It is commonly referred to simply as '''Ōtsuka Kofun'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
== Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a large, two-tiered round tumulus with a diameter of 53.4 meters and a height of 6.2 meters{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.
In 1965 (Showa 40), the burial chamber was looted{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. While the details remain unclear, the chamber is believed to have been a horizontal stone chamber made of tuff slabs. The estimated dimensions are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial artifacts remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was designated a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳}}.
== Surrounding Environment == [[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]] Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves}}. There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.
To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes. ** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
'''By Car''' * From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}): ** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.

'''Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (戸祭大塚古墳) is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} located in Kamitomatsuri-chō, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. It is commonly referred to simply as '''Ōtsuka Kofun'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
== Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a large, two-tiered round tumulus with a diameter of 53.4 meters and a height of 6.2 meters{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.
In 1965 (Showa 40), the burial chamber was looted{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. While the details remain unclear, the chamber is believed to have been a horizontal stone chamber made of tuff slabs. The estimated dimensions are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial artifacts remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was designated a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳}}.
== Surrounding Environment == [[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]] Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves}}. There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.
To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes. ** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
'''By Car''' * From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}): ** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Tomatsuri Otsuka Kofun.jpg|250px]] | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Kamitomatsuri-chō 2982-1{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|27|N|139|52|26|E|map_size:20000}} | type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | area = Diameter: 53.4 m, Height: 6.2 m{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | built = Late 6th century{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Ōtsuka Kofun) }}
'''Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (戸祭大塚古墳) is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} located in Kamitomatsuri-chō, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. It is commonly referred to simply as '''Ōtsuka Kofun'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
== Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a large, two-tiered round tumulus with a diameter of 53.4 meters and a height of 6.2 meters{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.
In 1965 (Showa 40), the burial chamber was looted{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. While the details remain unclear, the chamber is believed to have been a horizontal stone chamber made of tuff slabs. The estimated dimensions are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial artifacts remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was designated a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳}}.
== Surrounding Environment == [[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]] Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves}}. There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.
To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes. ** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
'''By Car''' * From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}): ** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|author=Hanawa Shizuo|title=Utsunomiya Rekishi Tanbō: Shiseki Annai Kyūjūkyūkei|publisher=Zuisōsha|date=2008-09-27|isbn=978-4-88748-179-4|ref={{sfnref|Hanawa|2008}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Utsunomiya City|title=Revised History of Utsunomiya|publisher=Utsunomiya City|date=1992-03-31|ncid=BN07977757|ref={{sfnref|Utsunomiya City Board|1992}}}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Tochigi Prefecture-designated cultural properties|ja|栃木県指定文化財一覧}} * {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}} * {{ill|Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}} * {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}} * {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} * {{ill|Shimo-Kuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|下栗大塚古墳}}

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Tomatsuri Otsuka Kofun.jpg|250px]] | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Kamitomatsuri-chō 2982-1{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|27|N|139|52|26|E|map_size:20000}} | type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | area = Diameter: 53.4 m, Height: 6.2 m{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | built = Late 6th century{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Ōtsuka Kofun) }}
'''Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (戸祭大塚古墳) is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} located in Kamitomatsuri-chō, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. It is commonly referred to simply as '''Ōtsuka Kofun'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
== Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a large, two-tiered round tumulus with a diameter of 53.4 meters and a height of 6.2 meters{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.
In 1965 (Showa 40), the burial chamber was looted{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. While the details remain unclear, the chamber is believed to have been a horizontal stone chamber made of tuff slabs. The estimated dimensions are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial artifacts remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was designated a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳}}.
== Surrounding Environment == [[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]] Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves}}. There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.
To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes. ** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
'''By Car''' * From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}): ** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Tomatsuri Otsuka Kofun.jpg|250px]] | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Kamitomatsuri-chō 2982-1{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|27|N|139|52|26|E|map_size:20000}} | type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | area = Diameter: 53.4 m, Height: 6.2 m{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | built = Late 6th century{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Ōtsuka Kofun) }}
'''Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (戸祭大塚古墳) is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} located in Kamitomatsuri-chō, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. It is commonly referred to simply as '''Ōtsuka Kofun'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
== Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a large, two-tiered round tumulus with a diameter of 53.4 meters and a height of 6.2 meters{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.
In 1965 (Showa 40), the burial chamber was looted{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. While the details remain unclear, the chamber is believed to have been a horizontal stone chamber made of tuff slabs. The estimated dimensions are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial artifacts remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was designated a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳}}.
== Surrounding Environment == [[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]] Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves}}. There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.
To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes. ** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
'''By Car''' * From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}): ** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.

'''Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (戸祭大塚古墳) is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} located in Kamitomatsuri-chō, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. It is commonly referred to simply as '''Ōtsuka Kofun'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
== Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a large, two-tiered round tumulus with a diameter of 53.4 meters and a height of 6.2 meters{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.
In 1965 (Showa 40), the burial chamber was looted{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. While the details remain unclear, the chamber is believed to have been a horizontal stone chamber made of tuff slabs. The estimated dimensions are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial artifacts remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was designated a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳}}.
== Surrounding Environment == [[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]] Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves}}. There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.
To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes. ** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
'''By Car''' * From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}): ** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.

'''Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (戸祭大塚古墳) is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} located in Kamitomatsuri-chō, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. It is commonly referred to simply as '''Ōtsuka Kofun'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
== Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a large, two-tiered round tumulus with a diameter of 53.4 meters and a height of 6.2 meters{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.
In 1965 (Showa 40), the burial chamber was looted{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. While the details remain unclear, the chamber is believed to have been a horizontal stone chamber made of tuff slabs. The estimated dimensions are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial artifacts remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was designated a Tochigi Prefecture other_designation along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳}}.
== Surrounding Environment == [[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]] Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves}}. There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.
To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes. ** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
'''By Car''' * From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}): ** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.

'''Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (戸祭大塚古墳) is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} located in Kamitomatsuri-chō, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. It is commonly referred to simply as '''Ōtsuka Kofun'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
== Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a large, two-tiered round tumulus with a diameter of 53.4 meters and a height of 6.2 meters{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.
In 1965 (Showa 40), the burial chamber was looted{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. While the details remain unclear, the chamber is believed to have been a horizontal stone chamber made of tuff slabs. The estimated dimensions are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial artifacts remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was designated a Tochigi Prefecture other_designation along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳}}.
== Surrounding Environment == [[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]] Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves}}. There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.
To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes. ** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
'''By Car''' * From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}): ** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.

'''Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (戸祭大塚古墳) is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} located in Kamitomatsuri-chō, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. It is commonly referred to simply as '''Ōtsuka Kofun'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
== Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a large, two-tiered round tumulus with a diameter of 53.4 meters and a height of 6.2 meters{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.
In 1965 (Showa 40), the burial chamber was looted{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. While the details remain unclear, the chamber is believed to have been a horizontal stone chamber made of tuff slabs. The estimated dimensions are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial artifacts remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was designated a Tochigi Prefecture other_designation along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳}}.
== Surrounding Environment == [[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]] Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves}}. There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.
To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes. ** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
'''By Car''' * From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}): ** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.

'''Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (戸祭大塚古墳) is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} located in Kamitomatsuri-chō, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. It is commonly referred to simply as '''Ōtsuka Kofun'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
== Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a large, two-tiered round tumulus with a diameter of 53.4 meters and a height of 6.2 meters{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.
In 1965 (Showa 40), the burial chamber was looted{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. While the details remain unclear, the chamber is believed to have been a horizontal stone chamber made of tuff slabs. The estimated dimensions are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial artifacts remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was designated a Tochigi Prefecture other_designation along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳}}.
== Surrounding Environment == [[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]] Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves}}. There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.
To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes. ** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
'''By Car''' * From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}): ** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.

'''Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (戸祭大塚古墳) is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} located in Kamitomatsuri-chō, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. It is commonly referred to simply as '''Ōtsuka Kofun'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
== Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a large, two-tiered round tumulus with a diameter of 53.4 meters and a height of 6.2 meters{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.
In 1965 (Showa 40), the burial chamber was looted{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. While the details remain unclear, the chamber is believed to have been a horizontal stone chamber made of tuff slabs. The estimated dimensions are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial burial goods remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was designation1 a Tochigi Prefecture other_designation along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳}}.
== Surrounding Environment == [[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]] Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves}}. There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.
To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes. ** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
'''By Car''' * From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}): ** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.

'''Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (戸祭大塚古墳) is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} located in Kamitomatsuri-chō, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. It is commonly referred to simply as '''Ōtsuka Kofun'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
== Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a large, two-tiered round tumulus with a diameter of 53.4 meters and a height of 6.2 meters{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.
In 1965 (Showa 40), the burial chamber was looted{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. While the details remain unclear, the chamber is believed to have been a horizontal stone chamber made of tuff slabs. The estimated area are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial burial goods remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was designation1 a Tochigi Prefecture other_designation along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳}}.
== Surrounding Environment == [[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]] Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves}}. There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.
To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes. ** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
'''By Car''' * From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}): ** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Tomatsuri Otsuka Kofun.jpg|250px]] | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Kamitomatsuri-chō 2982-1{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|27|N|139|52|26|E|map_size:20000}} | type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | area = Diameter: 53.4 m, Height: 6.2 m{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | built = Late 6th century{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}} | Buried Figure = | burial goods = | Designation = Tochigi Prefecture Designated other_designation (Ōtsuka Kofun) }}
'''Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (戸祭大塚古墳) is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} located in Kamitomatsuri-chō, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. It is commonly referred to simply as '''Ōtsuka Kofun'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
== Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a large, two-tiered round tumulus with a diameter of 53.4 meters and a height of 6.2 meters{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.
In 1965 (Showa 40), the burial chamber was looted{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. While the details remain unclear, the chamber is believed to have been a horizontal stone chamber made of tuff slabs. The estimated area are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial burial goods remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was designation1 a Tochigi Prefecture other_designation along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳}}.
== Surrounding Environment == [[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]] Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves}}. There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.
To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes. ** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
'''By Car''' * From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}): ** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|author=Hanawa Shizuo|title=Utsunomiya Rekishi Tanbō: Shiseki Annai Kyūjūkyūkei|publisher=Zuisōsha|date=2008-09-27|isbn=978-4-88748-179-4|ref={{sfnref|Hanawa|2008}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Utsunomiya City|title=Revised History of Utsunomiya|publisher=Utsunomiya City|date=1992-03-31|ncid=BN07977757|ref={{sfnref|Utsunomiya City Board|1992}}}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Tochigi Prefecture-designation1 cultural properties|ja|栃木県指定文化財一覧}} * {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}} * {{ill|Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}} * {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}} * {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} * {{ill|Shimo-Kuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|下栗大塚古墳}}

'''Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (戸祭大塚古墳) is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} located in Kamitomatsuri-chō, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. It is commonly referred to simply as '''Ōtsuka Kofun'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
== Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a large, two-tiered round tumulus with a diameter of 53.4 meters and a height of 6.2 meters{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.
In 1965 (Showa 40), the burial chamber was looted{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. While the details remain unclear, the chamber is believed to have been a horizontal stone chamber made of tuff slabs. The estimated area are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial burial goods remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was other_designation1 a Tochigi Prefecture other_other_designation along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳}}.
== Surrounding Environment == [[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]] Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves}}. There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.
To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes. ** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
'''By Car''' * From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}): ** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.

'''Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (戸祭大塚古墳) is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} located in Kamitomatsuri-chō, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. It is commonly referred to simply as '''Ōtsuka Kofun'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
== Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a large, two-tiered round tumulus with a diameter of 53.4 meters and a height of 6.2 meters{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.
In 1965 (Showa 40), the burial chamber was looted{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. While the details remain unclear, the chamber is believed to have been a horizontal stone chamber made of tuff slabs. The estimated area are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial burial goods remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was other_designation1 a Tochigi Prefecture other_other_designation along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳}}.
== Surrounding Environment == [[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]] Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves}}. There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.
To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes. ** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
'''By Car''' * From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}): ** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.

'''Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (戸祭大塚古墳) is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} located in Kamitomatsuri-chō, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. It is commonly referred to simply as '''Ōtsuka Kofun'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
== Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a large, two-tiered round tumulus with a diameter of 53.4 meters and a height of 6.2 meters{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.
In 1965 (Showa 40), the burial chamber was looted{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. While the details remain unclear, the chamber is believed to have been a horizontal stone chamber made of tuff slabs. The estimated area are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial burial goods remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was other_designation1 a Tochigi Prefecture other_other_designation along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳}}.
== Surrounding Environment == [[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]] Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves}}. There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.
To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes. ** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
'''By Car''' * From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}): ** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.

'''Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (戸祭大塚古墳) is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} located in Kamitomatsuri-chō, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. It is commonly referred to simply as '''Ōtsuka Kofun'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
== Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a large, two-tiered round tumulus with a diameter of 53.4 meters and a height of 6.2 meters{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.
In 1965 (Showa 40), the burial chamber was looted{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. While the details remain unclear, the chamber is believed to have been a horizontal stone chamber made of tuff slabs. The estimated area are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial burial goods remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was other_designation1 a Tochigi Prefecture other_other_designation along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳}}.
== Surrounding Environment == [[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]] Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves}}. There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.
To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes. ** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
'''By Car''' * From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}): ** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.

'''Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (戸祭大塚古墳) is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} located in Kamitomatsuri-chō, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. It is commonly referred to simply as '''Ōtsuka Kofun'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
== Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a large, two-tiered round tumulus with a diameter of 53.4 meters and a height of 6.2 meters{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.
In 1965 (Showa 40), the burial chamber was looted{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. While the details remain unclear, the chamber is believed to have been a horizontal stone chamber made of tuff slabs. The estimated area are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial burial goods remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was other_designation1 a Tochigi Prefecture other_designation along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳}}.
== Surrounding Environment == [[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]] Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves}}. There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.
To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes. ** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.
'''By Car''' * From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}): ** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.
