== Surrounding Environment ==
[[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]]
Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri-yama|qq=}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tone River System)|qq=}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taniguchi-yama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135490863}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}}. There are also remnants from the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.

To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.

== Access ==
'''By Bus'''
* From [[:en:Utsunomiya Station|Utsunomiya Station]] (JR):
** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus|1=Kantō Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655984}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes.
** Alternatively, take a bus bound for [[:en:Nikkō|Nikkō]] and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.

'''By Car'''
* From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Ring Road|1=Utsunomiya Ring Road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449792}}):
** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.

'''Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (戸祭大塚古墳) is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in Kamitomatsuri-chō, [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]], Tochigi Prefecture{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. It is commonly referred to simply as '''Ōtsuka Kofun'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.

== Overview ==
Ōtsuka Kofun is a large, two-tiered round tumulus with a diameter of 53.4 meters and a height of 6.2 meters{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.

In 1965 (Showa 40), the burial chamber was looted{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. While the details remain unclear, the chamber is believed to have been a horizontal stone chamber made of tuff slabs. The estimated area are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial burial goods remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.

On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was Historic Site1 a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasazuka Kofun|qq=}}.

== Surrounding Environment ==
[[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]]
Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri-yama|qq=}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tone River System)|qq=}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taniguchi-yama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135490863}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}}. There are also remnants from the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.

To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.

== Access ==
'''By Bus'''
* From [[:en:Utsunomiya Station|Utsunomiya Station]] (JR):
** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus|1=Kantō Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655984}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes.
** Alternatively, take a bus bound for [[:en:Nikkō|Nikkō]] and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.

'''By Car'''
* From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Ring Road|1=Utsunomiya Ring Road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449792}}):
** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.

== Surrounding Environment ==
[[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]]
Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri-yama|qq=}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tone River System)|qq=}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taniguchi-yama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135490863}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}}. There are also remnants from the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.

To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.

== Access ==
'''By Bus'''
* From [[:en:Utsunomiya Station|Utsunomiya Station]] (JR):
** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus|1=Kantō Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655984}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes.
** Alternatively, take a bus bound for [[:en:Nikkō|Nikkō]] and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.

'''By Car'''
* From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Ring Road|1=Utsunomiya Ring Road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449792}}):
** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.

'''Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (戸祭大塚古墳) is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in Kamitomatsuri-chō, [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]], Tochigi Prefecture{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. It is commonly referred to simply as '''Ōtsuka Kofun'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.

== Overview ==
Ōtsuka Kofun is a large, two-tiered round tumulus with a diameter of 53.4 meters and a height of 6.2 meters{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.

In 1965 (Showa 40), the burial chamber was looted{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. While the details remain unclear, the chamber is believed to have been a horizontal stone chamber made of tuff slabs. The estimated area are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial burial goods remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.

On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was Historic Site1 a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasazuka Kofun|qq=}}.

== Surrounding Environment ==
[[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]]
Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri-yama|qq=}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tone River System)|qq=}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taniguchi-yama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135490863}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}}. There are also remnants from the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.

To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.

== Access ==
'''By Bus'''
* From [[:en:Utsunomiya Station|Utsunomiya Station]] (JR):
** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus|1=Kantō Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655984}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes.
** Alternatively, take a bus bound for [[:en:Nikkō|Nikkō]] and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.

'''By Car'''
* From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Ring Road|1=Utsunomiya Ring Road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449792}}):
** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.

It is commonly referred to simply as '''Ōtsuka Kofun'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.

== Overview ==
Ōtsuka Kofun is a large, two-tiered round tumulus with a diameter of 53.4 meters and a height of 6.2 meters{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.

In 1965 (Showa 40), the burial chamber was looted{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. While the details remain unclear, the chamber is believed to have been a horizontal stone chamber made of tuff slabs. The estimated area are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial burial goods remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.

On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was Historic Site1 a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasazuka Kofun|qq=}}.

== Surrounding Environment ==
[[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]]
Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri-yama|qq=}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tone River System)|qq=}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taniguchi-yama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135490863}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}}. There are also remnants from the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.

To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.

== Access ==
'''By Bus'''
* From [[:en:Utsunomiya Station|Utsunomiya Station]] (JR):
** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus|1=Kantō Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655984}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes.
** Alternatively, take a bus bound for [[:en:Nikkō|Nikkō]] and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.

'''By Car'''
* From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Ring Road|1=Utsunomiya Ring Road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449792}}):
** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.

'''Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (戸祭大塚古墳) is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in Kamitomatsuri-chō, [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]], Tochigi Prefecture{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. It is commonly referred to simply as '''Ōtsuka Kofun'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.

== Overview ==
Ōtsuka Kofun is a large, two-tiered round tumulus with a diameter of 53.4 meters and a height of 6.2 meters{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.

In 1965 (Showa 40), the burial chamber was looted{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. While the details remain unclear, the chamber is believed to have been a horizontal stone chamber made of tuff slabs. The estimated area are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial burial goods remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.

On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was Historic Site1 a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasazuka Kofun|qq=}}.

== Surrounding Environment ==
[[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]]
Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri-yama|qq=}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tone River System)|qq=}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taniguchi-yama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135490863}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}}. There are also remnants from the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.

To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.

== Access ==
'''By Bus'''
* From [[:en:Utsunomiya Station|Utsunomiya Station]] (JR):
** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus|1=Kantō Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655984}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes.
** Alternatively, take a bus bound for [[:en:Nikkō|Nikkō]] and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.

'''By Car'''
* From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Ring Road|1=Utsunomiya Ring Road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449792}}):
** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.

== Surrounding Environment ==
[[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]]
Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri-yama|qq=}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tone River System)|qq=}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taniguchi-yama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135490863}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}}. There are also remnants from the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.

To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.

== Access ==
'''By Bus'''
* From [[:en:Utsunomiya Station|Utsunomiya Station]] (JR):
** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus|1=Kantō Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655984}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes.
** Alternatively, take a bus bound for [[:en:Nikkō|Nikkō]] and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.

'''By Car'''
* From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Ring Road|1=Utsunomiya Ring Road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449792}}):
** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.

'''Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (戸祭大塚古墳) is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in Kamitomatsuri-chō, [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]], Tochigi Prefecture{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. It is commonly referred to simply as '''Ōtsuka Kofun'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.

== Overview ==
Ōtsuka Kofun is a large, two-tiered round tumulus with a diameter of 53.4 meters and a height of 6.2 meters{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.

In 1965 (Showa 40), the burial chamber was looted{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. While the details remain unclear, the chamber is believed to have been a horizontal stone chamber made of tuff slabs. The estimated area are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial burial goods remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.

On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was Historic Site1 a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasazuka Kofun|qq=}}.

== Surrounding Environment ==
[[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]]
Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri-yama|qq=}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tone River System)|qq=}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taniguchi-yama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135490863}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}}. There are also remnants from the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.

To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.

== Access ==
'''By Bus'''
* From [[:en:Utsunomiya Station|Utsunomiya Station]] (JR):
** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus|1=Kantō Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655984}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes.
** Alternatively, take a bus bound for [[:en:Nikkō|Nikkō]] and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.

'''By Car'''
* From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Ring Road|1=Utsunomiya Ring Road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449792}}):
** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.

The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.

The estimated area are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. == Surrounding Environment ==
[[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]]
Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri-yama|qq=}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tone River System)|qq=}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taniguchi-yama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135490863}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}}. There are also remnants from the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.

To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.

== Access ==
'''By Bus'''
* From [[:en:Utsunomiya Station|Utsunomiya Station]] (JR):
** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus|1=Kantō Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655984}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes.
** Alternatively, take a bus bound for [[:en:Nikkō|Nikkō]] and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.

'''By Car'''
* From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Ring Road|1=Utsunomiya Ring Road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449792}}):
** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.

'''Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (戸祭大塚古墳) is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in Kamitomatsuri-chō, [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]], Tochigi Prefecture{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. It is commonly referred to simply as '''Ōtsuka Kofun'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.

== Overview ==
Ōtsuka Kofun is a large, two-tiered round tumulus with a diameter of 53.4 meters and a height of 6.2 meters{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.

In 1965 (Showa 40), the burial chamber was looted{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. While the details remain unclear, the chamber is believed to have been a horizontal stone chamber made of tuff slabs. The estimated area are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial burial goods remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.

On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was Historic Site1 a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasazuka Kofun|qq=}}.

== Surrounding Environment ==
[[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]]
Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri-yama|qq=}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tone River System)|qq=}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taniguchi-yama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135490863}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}}. There are also remnants from the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.

To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.

== Access ==
'''By Bus'''
* From [[:en:Utsunomiya Station|Utsunomiya Station]] (JR):
** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus|1=Kantō Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655984}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes.
** Alternatively, take a bus bound for [[:en:Nikkō|Nikkō]] and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.

'''By Car'''
* From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Ring Road|1=Utsunomiya Ring Road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449792}}):
** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.

'''Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (戸祭大塚古墳) is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in Kamitomatsuri-chō, {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|Utsunomiya|WD=Q200589|lt=Utsunomiya}}, Tochigi Prefecture{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. It is commonly referred to simply as '''Ōtsuka Kofun'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.

== Overview ==
Ōtsuka Kofun is a large, two-tiered round tumulus with a diameter of 53.4 meters and a height of 6.2 meters{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.

In 1965 (Showa 40), the burial chamber was looted{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. While the details remain unclear, the chamber is believed to have been a horizontal stone chamber made of tuff slabs. The estimated area are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial burial goods remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.

On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was Historic Site1 a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasazuka Kofun|qq=}}.

== Surrounding Environment ==
[[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]]
Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri-yama|qq=}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tone River System)|qq=}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taniguchi-yama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135490863}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}}. There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.

To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.

== Access ==
'''By Bus'''
* From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|en|Utsunomiya Station|WD=Q801549|lt=Utsunomiya Station}} (JR):
** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus|1=Kantō Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655984}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes.
** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|en|Nikkō|WD=Q235753|lt=Nikkō}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.

'''By Car'''
* From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Ring Road|1=Utsunomiya Ring Road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449792}}):
** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.

'''Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (戸祭大塚古墳) is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in Kamitomatsuri-chō, {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|Utsunomiya|WD=Q200589|lt=Utsunomiya}}, Tochigi Prefecture{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. It is commonly referred to simply as '''Ōtsuka Kofun'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.

== Overview ==
Ōtsuka Kofun is a large, two-tiered round tumulus with a diameter of 53.4 meters and a height of 6.2 meters{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.

In 1965 (Showa 40), the burial chamber was looted{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. While the details remain unclear, the chamber is believed to have been a horizontal stone chamber made of tuff slabs. The estimated area are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial burial goods remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.

On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was Historic Site1 a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasazuka Kofun|qq=}}.

== Surrounding Environment ==
[[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]]
Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri-yama|qq=}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tone River System)|qq=}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taniguchi-yama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135490863}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}}. There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.

To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.

== Access ==
'''By Bus'''
* From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|en|Utsunomiya Station|WD=Q801549|lt=Utsunomiya Station}} (JR):
** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus|1=Kantō Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655984}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes.
** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|en|Nikkō|WD=Q235753|lt=Nikkō}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.

'''By Car'''
* From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Ring Road|1=Utsunomiya Ring Road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449792}}):
** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.

'''Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (戸祭大塚古墳) is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in Kamitomatsuri-chō, {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|Utsunomiya|WD=Q200589|lt=Utsunomiya}}, Tochigi Prefecture{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. It is commonly referred to simply as '''Ōtsuka Kofun'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.

== Overview ==
Ōtsuka Kofun is a large, two-tiered round tumulus with a diameter of 53.4 meters and a height of 6.2 meters{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.

In 1965 (Showa 40), the burial chamber was looted{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. While the details remain unclear, the chamber is believed to have been a horizontal stone chamber made of tuff slabs. The estimated area are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial burial goods remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.

On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was Historic Site1 a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasazuka Kofun|qq=}}.

== Surrounding Environment ==
[[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]]
Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri-yama|qq=}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tone River System)|qq=}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taniguchi-yama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135490863}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}}. There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.

To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.

== Access ==
'''By Bus'''
* From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|en|Utsunomiya Station|WD=Q801549|lt=Utsunomiya Station}} (JR):
** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus|1=Kantō Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655984}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes.
** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|en|Nikkō|WD=Q235753|lt=Nikkō}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.

'''By Car'''
* From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Ring Road|1=Utsunomiya Ring Road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449792}}):
** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}

'''Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (戸祭大塚古墳) is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in Kamitomatsuri-chō, {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|Utsunomiya|WD=Q200589|lt=Utsunomiya}}, Tochigi Prefecture{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. It is commonly referred to simply as '''Ōtsuka Kofun'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.

== Overview ==
Ōtsuka Kofun is a large, two-tiered round tumulus with a diameter of 53.4 meters and a height of 6.2 meters{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.

In 1965 (Showa 40), the burial chamber was looted{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. While the details remain unclear, the chamber is believed to have been a horizontal stone chamber made of tuff slabs. The estimated area are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial burial goods remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.

On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was Historic Site1 a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasazuka Kofun|qq=}}.

== Surrounding Environment ==
[[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]]
Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri-yama|qq=}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tone River System)|qq=}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taniguchi-yama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135490863}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}}. There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.

To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.

== Access ==
'''By Bus'''
* From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|en|Utsunomiya Station|WD=Q801549|lt=Utsunomiya Station}} (JR):
** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus|1=Kantō Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655984}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes.
** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|en|Nikkō|WD=Q235753|lt=Nikkō}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.

'''By Car'''
* From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Ring Road|1=Utsunomiya Ring Road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449792}}):
** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.

'''By Bus'''

** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus|1=Kantō Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655984}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes.
** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|en|Nikkō|WD=Q235753|lt=Nikkō}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.

'''By Car'''

** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}

| type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}
| area = Diameter: 53.4 m, Height: 6.2 m{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}
| built = Late 6th century{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}

| architectural_styles = {{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}}

'''Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (戸祭大塚古墳) is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in Kamitomatsuri-chō, {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|Utsunomiya|WD=Q200589|lt=Utsunomiya|1=Utsunomiya}}, Tochigi Prefecture{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. It is commonly referred to simply as '''Ōtsuka Kofun'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.

== Overview ==
Ōtsuka Kofun is a large, two-tiered round tumulus with a diameter of 53.4 meters and a height of 6.2 meters{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.

In 1965 (Showa 40), the burial chamber was looted{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. While the details remain unclear, the chamber is believed to have been a horizontal stone chamber made of tuff slabs. The estimated area are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial burial goods remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.

On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was Historic Site1 a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasazuka Kofun|qq=}}.

== Surrounding Environment ==
[[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]]
Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri-yama|qq=}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tone River System)|qq=}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taniguchi-yama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135490863|1=Kitayama Kofun Cluster}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}}. There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.

To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.

== Access ==
'''By Bus'''
* From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|en|Utsunomiya Station|WD=Q801549|lt=Utsunomiya Station|1=Utsunomiya Station}} (JR):
** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus|1=Q11655984|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655984}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes.
** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|en|Nikkō|WD=Q235753|lt=Nikkō|1=Nikkō}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.

'''By Car'''
* From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Ring Road|1=Utsunomiya Ring Road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449792}}):
** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.

'''Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (戸祭大塚古墳) is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in Kamitomatsuri-chō, {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|Utsunomiya|WD=Q200589|lt=Utsunomiya|1=Utsunomiya}}, Tochigi Prefecture{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. It is commonly referred to simply as '''Ōtsuka Kofun'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.

== Overview ==
Ōtsuka Kofun is a large, two-tiered round tumulus with a diameter of 53.4 meters and a height of 6.2 meters{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The first tier is 3.4 meters high, topped by a second tier measuring 2.8 meters high{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. The flat summit of the second tier has a diameter of 9 meters{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Known for its elegant structure, it is one of the rare conical tumuli in Japan. A moat approximately 6.6 meters wide and 1.3 meters deep remains on the northwestern to eastern perimeter{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}.

In 1965 (Showa 40), the burial chamber was looted{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. While the details remain unclear, the chamber is believed to have been a horizontal stone chamber made of tuff slabs. The estimated area are 5.17 meters in length, 1.8–2.1 meters in width, and 1.75–2.14 meters in height. It is thought to date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} (6th century){{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}. Evidence suggests that additional burials occurred{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}. No records of burial burial goods remain{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}. Due to the emergency nature of the investigation following the looting{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}, the burial corridor has not been excavated{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.

On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Ōtsuka Kofun was Historic Site1 a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site along with {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun|ja|笹塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasazuka Kofun|qq=}}.

== Surrounding Environment ==
[[File:Ojino Kofuns.jpg|thumb|Ōjino Kofun Cluster]]
Ōtsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City Board|1992|p=55}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri-yama|qq=}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tone River System)|qq=}}. Nearby are numerous ancient tumuli and historical sites, including {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taniguchi-yama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135490863|1=Kitayama Kofun Cluster}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawara-zuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}}. There are also remnants from the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}, such as the {{ill|Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidō-yama Tile Kiln Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} has been developed to connect these historical locations.

To the north of Ōtsuka Kofun lies the Ōjino Kofun Cluster, consisting of several round mounds{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=107}}.

== Access ==
'''By Bus'''
* From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|en|Utsunomiya Station|WD=Q801549|lt=Utsunomiya Station|1=Utsunomiya Station}} (JR):
** Take a {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus|1=Q11655984|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655984}} on the Tomatsuridai Circular Line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Daini Greenhill North" and walk 10 minutes.
** Alternatively, take a bus bound for {{ill|Nikkō|en|Nikkō|WD=Q235753|lt=Nikkō|1=Nikkō}} and alight at "Nagaoka Kaidō Entrance," approximately 1 km via Nagaoka Kaidō{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=106}}.

'''By Car'''
* From National Route 119 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Ring Road|1=Utsunomiya Ring Road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449792}}):
** Turn south at the Nagaoka-machi Intersection for 1 minute or east at the Kamitomatsuri-chō Intersection for 1 minute. Follow the branch road eastward for 1 minute to Nagaoka Kaidō.
